name: Kene Nwangwu
headline: Return-game specialist with no real backfield path in New York
body:
Nwangwu's profile is a special-teams story, not an offensive one. His 2025 advanced line lays it bare: 13 carries for 49 yards (3.77 per attempt) on a 4.4 percent snap share across 10 games, with a single target, a -0.288 EPA per play, and just 10.9 fantasy points. He created almost nothing on his own, logging 1.92 yards after contact per attempt, zero broken tackles, and only one explosive run. That mirrors his thin 2023 sample in Minnesota, when he managed 2.6 yards a pop on five totes.

The Jets brought him in on a modest one-year deal explicitly for return duties, and nothing about the depth chart points to a scrimmage role. Ranked outside RB179 in consensus drafts and rostered in essentially no leagues, he carries zero seasonal value. Only an injury ahead of him would change the math; until then, leave him on waivers and revisit only if New York's backfield collapses.
